fields.description:
## Why

The 2026-07-08 principal-architect audit (Reliability finding #3) found that three documented reconciler safety mechanisms are implemented, tested, and **never called in production** - the safety they promise is illusory:

1. **Create->label crash window double-creates Jira issues.** `dispatch_one.py:241-256` runs `create_issue` -> adds the `rebar-id:` label the JQL dedup keys on -> binds in memory only; bindings persist only at end of pass. A hard crash between create and label leaves an orphan issue invisible to dedup; the re-run duplicates it.
2. **The write-ahead/recovery protocol is dead code - and miswired.** `bind_pending` has zero production callers, and `run_differs.py:351` passes the applier MODULE (no `search_issues`) to `recover_pending_bindings`, so real recovery `AttributeError`s into a fail-open swallow.
3. **Crashed pass deadlocks reconciliation forever.** Lease `steal()` is implemented/tested (`_ref_lock.py:516-535`) but the held-ref path unconditionally exits 3 (`__main__.py:499-505`); after SIGKILL the remote `refs/reconciler/lock` persists until an operator hand-deletes it.
4. **The wrapped write path never retries transient HTTP failures.** The REST helper re-raises every `HTTPError` (`acli_rest.py:61-63`) and `_call_with_retry` (`dispatch_one.py:100-144`) only retries `JiraAPIError`, which the raw-`HTTPError` REST path never raises. C4 fixes only the bulk-path 429 parity, not the wrapped write path.

## What

Wire each mechanism into its intended call site, smallest-change-first (see Design decisions D1-D4 and Sequencing).

1. **Revive the retry layer WITHOUT a boundary translation.** Extend `_call_with_retry` (dispatch_one.py:100-144) to ALSO recognize a retryable `urllib.error.HTTPError` (429; 5xx) in addition to `JiraAPIError`, leaving acli_rest's raw-`HTTPError` raise UNCHANGED (8+ raw-HTTPError catchers rely on it). Non-retryable 4xx propagate raw. Backoff formula per ADR 0036: honor `Retry-After` header when present; else `2**(attempt+1) + rand[0,1)` seconds, capped at 60s; max_retries=3 (matching the existing `delays=[1,2,4]` cardinality).
2. **Write-safety via a per-retry dedup guard on CREATE only.** Wrap `create_issue` in a dedup-aware callable that RE-RUNS the rebar-id JQL search at the START of every retry attempt and short-circuits to the existing issue if the key now exists. Comments are scoped OUT (D2). Idempotent ops (update/transition/label) retry directly.
3. **Deterministic write-ahead recovery (D1).** Persist a `bind_pending` record BEFORE `create_issue`; once create returns a key, `record_pending_key(local_id, jira_key)` and persist AGAIN BEFORE `add_label`. Hoist the `AcliClient` (currently built at run_differs.py:393 for the outbound differ) ABOVE the recovery call and pass it (not the applier module) to `recover_pending_bindings`. Make recovery failures LOUD: `alert_store` bridge alert (the 24h-deduped fail-open channel) + a nonzero `recovery_failures` pass-result/`sync_pass_end` tally.
4. **Crashed-pass lease steal (D3).** In `__main__.py`'s held-lock path, attempt `steal()` (the sanctioned skew-proof expiry primitive) instead of unconditional exit 3, gated behind a kill-switch env (`REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_STEAL`, default ON).

## Scope

In:
- `dispatch_one.py`: `_call_with_retry` HTTPError-aware (429/5xx, ADR-0036 backoff); dedup-guarded `create_issue` callable; `bind_pending`+persist before create and `record_pending_key`+persist after create/before label; **unwrap `add_comment` from `_call_with_retry` at BOTH `:341` (create path) and `:610` (update path)** -> single-attempt bare call feeding the existing `comment_errors` list (D2).
- `run_differs.py`: hoist the `AcliClient` above the recovery call; fix `recover_pending_bindings` client; capture its `(recovered, failures)` return; emit `alert_store` alert on failure.
- `binding_store.py`: add `record_pending_key(local_id, jira_key) -> None` (sets `jira_key` on an existing pending entry, keeps `state='pending'`, persists via `save()`); `recover_pending_bindings` returns `(recovered: int, failures: int)` and gains the deterministic branch (confirm a keyed-pending record with NO Jira search; keyless -> `search_issues` fallback); update the module docstring to record the keyed-pending invariant (D1 / addresses the ADR-note finding).
- `reconcile.py`: thread `recovery_failures` into the pass `result` dict (beside `mutation_failures`) and the `sync_pass_end` log.
- `__main__.py`: hoist `pass_id` generation ABOVE the `check_pass_lock` block so it is the `steal()` holder; add `steal_pass_lock` in the held-lock path with heartbeat wiring (B3); `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_STEAL` kill-switch.
- `_advisory_lock.py`: thin `steal_pass_lock(holder, repo_root, *, sleep_fn=time.sleep) -> str | None` wrapping `_ref_lock.steal(repo_root, LOCK_REF, holder=holder, remote=..., sleep_fn=sleep_fn)`.
- Tests under `tests/scripts/bridge/` and `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/`.

Read-only (relied on, not changed): `acli_rest.py` (raw HTTPError raise UNCHANGED); `_ref_lock.py` (`steal()` already implemented).

Out (justified): bulk-path 429 parity + `_call_with_backoff` (C4 owns); inbound status mapping (audit-remediation epic). **Deferred with rationale** (see Findings triage): unresolvable-pending TTL/retirement; live end-to-end reconciler run; `recovery_failures` as an exit-code gate.

## Design decisions (round 4, resolved from code facts)

- **D1 - create->label window closed DETERMINISTICALLY, not by fuzzy search.** acli `create_issue` cannot carry the rebar-id label atomically (no-priority path has no `--label`; `--labels` is destructive SET-REPLACE). Instead the pending binding records the `jira_key` the instant `create_issue` returns (persisted before `add_label`), so `recover_pending_bindings` confirms a keyed pending record with NO Jira search - deterministic. Only a keyless pending record (crash before/during create) uses the label `search_issues` fallback. **bind_pending persist failure is FATAL for that create** (abort + loud error): proceeding without a durable pending record reintroduces the exact orphan window. **Rollback/back-compat:** a keyed-pending record (`state='pending'`, non-null `jira_key`) is additive to the existing schema; an OLD (pre-plan) reconciler treats it as an ordinary pending and label-searches (which still resolves, since the label was attached before the binding would have confirmed) - no corruption. Setter: `record_pending_key(local_id, jira_key)`.
- **D2 - comments scoped OUT of the retry/dedup guard.** No cheap Jira-side idempotency key exists for a comment (posted verbatim through the `acli_subprocess` floor - `acli_cli_ops.py:491`), and a duplicate comment is low-harm vs. a duplicate issue; a visible `[rebar-cid]` trailer would pollute every synced comment. So `add_comment` is unwrapped from `_call_with_retry` at `dispatch_one.py:341` AND `:610` and posted with a SINGLE attempt; a failed post is recorded to the existing non-fatal `comment_errors` list and re-emitted by the comment differ next pass (eventually-consistent). The real failure surfaced by `add_comment` is `AcliMutationError`/`RetryExhaustedError` (subprocess floor), NOT `urllib.error.HTTPError` - the test injects that real type.
- **D3 - lease expiry uses `steal()` itself, not a wall-clock `pass_lock_is_expired`.** `_ref_lock` treats `heartbeat_ns` as diagnostic-only and determines expiry behaviorally via `steal()` (read -> sleep one `lease_secs` -> re-check `(oid,fence)` progress). Held-lock path calls `advisory.steal_pass_lock(pass_id, repo_root)` -> `_ref_lock.steal()`:
  - returns a **new oid** -> lease was stale -> **steal won**: thread the new oid into `lock_oid` and start the heartbeat daemon against it (mirroring the acquire path at `__main__.py:544-549`), then proceed (B3).
  - returns **None** -> re-read the ref: if now **free** (holder released during our sleep) -> attempt a normal `acquire_pass_lock` (proceed if won, exit 3 if lost to a racer); if still **held** (live holder) -> **exit 3** (B1 / freed-ref reconciliation).
  `pass_id` is generated BEFORE the lock check so it is the steal `holder`. The steal sleeps one `lock_lease_secs` (default 120s, `_advisory_lock._lock_lease_secs`); a structured log line is emitted at the START of the sleep so operators observe the wait, and `sleep_fn` is injected in tests. A successful steal logs the stolen prior holder + new oid (`lease_steal` event). The whole steal branch is behind `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_STEAL` (default ON) - an ops kill-switch reverting to unconditional exit-3 without a deploy (back-out).
- **D4 - `recovery_failures` threading API.** `recover_pending_bindings(client) -> tuple[int, int]` returns `(recovered, failures)`. `run_differs` captures `failures`, stores it on the pass context/return, and `reconcile.py` reads it into the `result` dict (beside `mutation_failures`) and the `sync_pass_end` log. It is observability-only and intentionally NOT wired into `__main__.py`'s `mutation_failures` exit gate (`:345`): recovery is best-effort/fail-open by design, so a transient Jira search hiccup must not fail an otherwise-clean pass.

## Sequencing (why one change, addresses decomposition finding)

The four fixes are the single coherent "revive the dead reconciler safety machinery" workstream from the audit; each has its OWN independent unit test (below) and none depends on another's runtime, but they share one small reconciler test surface and touch overlapping files (`dispatch_one.py` for both retry+write-ahead). Landing them as one Gerrit change avoids four near-trivial stacked changes with repeated rebase/review overhead; each AC is independently verifiable, so a reviewer can accept/reject any fix on its own test. Fix 4 (lease steal) is additionally guarded by its own kill-switch, so it can be disabled in production independent of the others.

## Findings triage (round 4 review - deferrals justified)

- **Unresolvable-pending TTL/retirement** (adv, 0.264): DEFERRED. A pending record that cannot resolve stays pending and re-alerts each pass (24h-deduped); a TTL/abandon path is genuinely separate follow-up work, filed as a discovered-from ticket rather than expanding this change.
- **Live end-to-end AC** (adv, 0.0): DEFERRED. This ticket is code-only by the epic's decomposition; live reconciler exercise is owned by sibling D1 (close-ferreous-cardinal) and the reconcile-bridge GHA. Documented, not silently dropped.
- **`recovery_failures` exit gate** (adv, 0.0): INTENTIONAL non-gate - see D4 (fail-open by design).
- **JQL eventual-consistency TOCTOU** (adv, 0.0): ACKNOWLEDGED residual. Jira Cloud's JQL index lags, so a create that landed may miss the retry-path dedup search. The durable mitigation is D1's keyed-pending write-ahead (the next pass recovers via the recorded key with no search), which covers the cross-crash case; the in-pass residual (429 where the request landed but returned no key AND the index has not caught up) cannot be fully eliminated because Jira offers no create idempotency token - documented here rather than hidden.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `_call_with_retry` retries a retryable `HTTPError`: 429 honoring `Retry-After` when PRESENT and falling back to jittered exponential backoff (`2**(attempt+1)+rand[0,1)`, cap 60s) when ABSENT; 5xx with the same jittered backoff; non-retryable 4xx re-raised. WITHOUT translating at `acli_rest.py` - a test asserts the raw `HTTPError` raise from `acli_rest` is unchanged so the raw-`HTTPError` catchers (e.g. apply_handlers.py:151's 404 path) still see raw `HTTPError`. Both the header-present and header-absent 429 paths are tested. Proof: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_retry_httperror.py`.
- [ ] The non-idempotent **create** re-runs its rebar-id JQL dedup check at the START of every retry attempt and short-circuits to the existing issue if the key now exists - a test injects a retryable `HTTPError` 429 AFTER a `create_issue` that already landed and asserts NO duplicate issue is created. **Comments post with a single attempt** (D2): a test injects the real subprocess-floor failure (`AcliMutationError`/`RetryExhaustedError`) on `add_comment` and asserts `add_comment` is called EXACTLY once (unwrapped at both `:341` and `:610`) and the failure is recorded to `comment_errors` (no duplicate post). Proof: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_create_dedup_guard.py`.
- [ ] Write-ahead is deterministic (D1): `bind_pending(local_id)` is persisted BEFORE `create_issue`; on the returned key, `record_pending_key(local_id, jira_key)` persists BEFORE `add_label`. `recover_pending_bindings` confirms any keyed-pending record (retro-attaching the label idempotently) with NO Jira search -> a crash between create and label yields NO duplicate; a keyless pending record falls back to `search_issues` (confirm if found, else unbind). A `bind_pending` persist failure aborts the create loudly. Tests: (a) crash after create+key-persist, before label -> next pass confirms, no duplicate; (b) crash before create -> fallback unbind/relabel; (c) `bind_pending` persist failure aborts + alerts. Proof: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_write_ahead_recovery.py`.
- [ ] `recover_pending_bindings` receives the hoisted `AcliClient` (exposing `search_issues`, not the applier module) and returns `(recovered, failures)`; a recovery failure emits an `alert_store` bridge alert AND increments a nonzero `recovery_failures` tally on the pass `result` + `sync_pass_end` log, instead of the silent fail-open swallow. Proof: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_recovery_loud_failures.py`.
- [ ] Held-lock path (D3, behind `REBAR_RECONCILER_LOCK_STEAL`): a **stale** lease (steal returns a new oid) is stolen, the new oid is threaded into `lock_oid`, the heartbeat is started against it, and the pass proceeds; **None + still-held** (live holder) -> exit 3; **None + freed** (ref released during the sleep) -> normal `acquire_pass_lock` attempt. All three cases covered with an injected `sleep_fn`. Proof: `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_lock_steal.py`.
- [ ] Both test locations green: `python -m pytest tests/scripts/bridge/ -q` AND `python -m pytest tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ -q`.
